Plan your trip

Amalfi Coast Itineraries

1 day, 3 days or 1 week: detailed itineraries to make the most of the Amalfi Coast however much time you have.

The Amalfi Coast can be visited in one (intense) day or savoured for weeks. The difference is enormous: someone with just one day will see the iconic sights but rarely what lies beyond the postcard; someone with a week can discover the quieter villages, hike the trails, eat at local restaurants, and stop to watch the sunset. These itineraries have been designed to optimise every hour, whatever time you have available.

1 day

The best of the coast in one day

For those arriving from Naples or Salerno for the day. Intense but unforgettable.

08:00

Arrive in Amalfi by ferry

Take the morning ferry from Naples or Salerno. Arriving in Amalfi by sea is one of the most exciting moments of the trip. Drop your bags and explore the Cathedral square.

09:30

Sant'Andrea Cathedral & Valle dei Mulini

Visit the magnificent Cathedral (free access to the square, paid museum). Then walk the Valle dei Mulini to see the ancient paper mills. 1h30 in total.

11:30

Ferry to Positano

25 minutes of ferry with spectacular views. In Positano, climb to the Church of Santa Maria Assunta and enjoy the panorama over the coloured rooftops.

13:30

Lunch in Positano

Find a trattoria on the Via dei Mulini or along the promenade. Spaghetti alle vongole or linguine al limone are essential.

15:00

Spiaggia Grande

2 hours on the beach in Positano. The water is beautiful even in the afternoon. Hire a sun lounger and umbrella at a beach club.

18:00

Aperitivo and sunset

Positano has some of the finest terrace bars in Italy. A Campari Spritz watching the sun sink into the sea is worth the trip alone.

19:30

Return by bus or ferry

SITA bus to Sorrento (then Circumvesuviana to Naples) or evening ferry to Salerno or Naples, depending on your destination.

3 days

Three days on the coast

Ideal base: Amalfi or Praiano. Perfect balance between iconic sights and discoveries.

1 Day 1 — Amalfi and surroundings

  • Morning: Arrival and check-in. Visit Sant'Andrea Cathedral, Piazza del Duomo, Valle dei Mulini.
  • Lunch: Trattoria in the old town. Scialatielli ai frutti di mare.
  • Afternoon: Ferry to the Grotta dello Smeraldo at Conca dei Marini (45-minute round trip).
  • Evening: Dinner in Amalfi with artisan limoncello.

2 Day 2 — Positano and Praiano

  • Morning: Ferry to Positano. Visit Santa Maria Assunta Church, Via dei Mulini, shops.
  • Lunch: In Positano — even standing with a focaccia.
  • Afternoon: Bus to Praiano (15 minutes). Head down to Marina di Praia and swim in the turquoise cove.
  • Evening: Aperitivo in Praiano watching the sunset towards Positano.

3 Day 3 — Ravello and Cetara

  • Morning: Bus from Amalfi to Ravello (30 minutes). Villa Rufolo, belvedere terrace, old town.
  • Lunch: In Ravello, at a restaurant with a sea-view terrace.
  • Afternoon: Return to Amalfi and bus to Cetara (20 minutes). Visit the fishing village, taste the anchovy colatura.
  • Evening: Dinner in Cetara (prices much lower than Positano). Depart the next day.
1 week

One week on the coast

For those who want to go beyond the postcard. Hikes, hidden villages, local life.

Days 1–3

Follow the 3-day itinerary above. Take your time to explore the iconic sights without rushing.

Day 4 — Path of the Gods

The coast's most famous trek: from Bomerano (Agerola) to Nocelle above Positano. 7.8 km with unforgettable views. Moderate level, 3–4 hours.

Day 5 — Vietri and Salerno

Visit the ceramics workshops in Vietri sul Mare (one hour), then continue to Salerno: the seafront, the medieval old town, and the Diocesan Museum are excellent and little-known.

Day 6 — Boat trip

Rent a boat (with or without skipper) from Amalfi or Positano and explore the sea caves, stacks and coves inaccessible by land. One of the finest experiences on the coast.

Day 7 — Slow day

Choose your favourite beach, a good book, a restaurant for lunch and no programme. The coast is wonderful this way too.

How do I get to the coast?

All the options: train, ferry, bus and private transfers.

Transport guide

Your independent travel guide to the Amalfi Coast — honest, detailed, and updated regularly.

The Amalfi Coast — La Costiera Amalfitana
